description: A substance used for coloring; the material source of color before it becomes paint or dye. 'Kolai' names what holds color—earth, plant, or mineral ground to release hue.
sound_symbolism: The word colors bold. 'Ko' concentrates with intense presence, color gathered. 'La' spreads with radiating hue. Then 'i' rises to vivid staining.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo kolai' (the pigment). Raw color material. Ground for paint, mixed for 'tinoa' (dye).

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Color from earth—natural pigments creating the organic palette of Art Nouveau.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Pigments from nature—ochre, indigo, cochineal, colors gathered from the world.
